exemplar.title: To what extent has Kia's launch of their new product initiative: "Plan S" enabled them to increase their market share within the US electric vehicle (EV) market successfully?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has Kia's launch of their new product initiative: "Plan S" enabled them to increase their market share within the US electric vehicle (EV) market successfully?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Consistent framing around sustainability and clear use of Elkingtonâs TBL throughout the investigation
Strong links drawn between each sustainability pillar and Kiaâs market-share objectives
weaknesses: Occasional drift into generic commentary without always reconnecting to the research question
Some social-sustainability measures not clearly distinguished as part of Plan S
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work in band 4: the key concept of sustainability is systematically integrated via TBL, with concrete evidence in each pillar. Minor lapsesâgeneric EV trend commentary and occasional conceptual ambiguityâprevent full, seamless integration required for band 5, so a 4/5 is justified.

strengths: Four varied documents labelled in works cited and used in analysis
Materials provide relevant sales, financial and strategic data
weaknesses: Mismatch between cited and listed documents in the Supporting Documents section
Secondary sources tend to be descriptive rather than deeply analytical
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student meets the requirement for 3â5 relevant documents and generally uses them to support analysis, aligning with band 3. Depth and range of ideas are adequate but not extensive, and the citation mismatch prevents full marks.

strengths: Broad suite of tools selected (TBL, STEEPLE, SWOT, motivational theories, ratios)
Clear links drawn between most tools and the research focus
weaknesses: Misalignment in STEEPLE âsocialâ factor and Maslow mapping
Regression analysis mentioned without reporting equation or RÂ²
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the selection of tools is comprehensive, uneven mapping of theories and missing statistical details weaken the clarity of relevance. The application is adequate but not consistently effective, placing work at band 3 rather than the top band.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses up-to-date, varied data (sales figures, ratios, employee metrics) with clear sourcing and tabulation
Analysis goes beyond description to identify opportunities, weaknesses and compare trends with competitors
weaknesses: Key claims (e.g., recycled-plastic USP) lack quantitative substantiation
Several data points (gender pay gap, market-share increases) need better contextualisation or citation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection and use are sufficient and the discussion is coherent, displaying mostly effective analysis with integration of theory. Missing quantitative backing for some claims and incomplete citations prevent a top-band score, so 4/5 is appropriate.

strengths: Conclusions fully consistent with the evidence and summarize findings across all three TBL pillars
Explicitly and directly answers the research question regarding Plan Sâs effectiveness
weaknesses: Occasional overstatement (e.g., âdominate market shareâ) that outpaces the evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion aligns logically with the data and provides a clear, supported answer to the research question, fulfilling band 3. Minor overstated claims do not undermine overall consistency or completeness.

strengths: Logical overall structure: introduction, analysis sections, conclusion, appendices and TOC mirror each other
weaknesses: Incomplete sentence and occasional pagination/numbering errors interrupt flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: An appropriate and coherent structure is maintained throughout, matching the descriptor for full marks despite minor formatting lapses.

strengths: All required HL IA elements are present and correctly labelled
Visuals and tables are well integrated and sourced
weaknesses: TOC lacks page numbers and consistent formatting of entries
Typographical errors and inconsistent citation formatting remain
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission includes all mandatory presentation elements and meets top-band expectations, though small stylistic and formatting issues persist.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical structure is evident: introduction, three analysis sections, conclusion, references and appendices, all mirrored in the table of contents.  Headings and sub-headings aid navigation, and appendices are signposted.  Minor disjointed pagination and occasional numbering errors are present but do not impede overall flow, satisfying the descriptor for âappropriate structure throughout.â

The chosen documents are clearly relevant to the research question and provide sales, financial and strategic data that the student uses in the analysis.  Depth is generally adequate, though several secondary web sources are descriptive rather than analytical, and the range of ideas is moderate rather than extensive.  This aligns with ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ (band 3).

All required elements for the HL IAâtitle, word count, table of contents, bibliography, appendices, chartsâare present and appropriately labelled.  Visuals and tables are integrated and sourced.  Hence the work meets the criteria for the top presentation band (2 marks).

Four supporting documents are formally labelled in the âWorks Cited â Supporting Documentsâ section, satisfying the 3-5 requirement.  The documents are varied (industry report, three Kia quarterly results).  This places the work solidly in the 3-mark band for number of documents.

The tools are generally applied with clear relevance: profitability ratios are calculated and interpreted; Deci & Ryan is linked to staff satisfaction data; STEEPLE factors are tied to consumer behaviour.  While some interpretations remain descriptive, most applications directly illuminate how Plan S affects Kiaâs market share, demonstrating âclear relevance and effective applicationâ (band 4).

The investigation draws on quarterly EV-sales figures, calculated market-share percentages, profitability ratios, turnover rates and training-hour statistics.  Data are mostly up-to-date, sourced and tabulated in appendices, then referenced in the body text, satisfying the rubricâs requirement for âsufficient selection and useâ (band 4).  Minor gaps (e.g., lack of industry-wide profit comparisons) prevent a leap to the top band.

Analysis moves beyond description by identifying opportunities, weaknesses and long-term implications, and by comparing Kiaâs trajectory with Teslaâs declining share.  The discussion is mostly coherent, with integration of data and theory, though some evaluative points remain surface-level (e.g., brief treatment of gender pay gap impacts).  This corresponds to âmostly effective analysis with some integrationâ (band 4).

The student explicitly answers the research question by stating that Plan S was âto a large extent effectiveâ in increasing Kiaâs US EV market share, supported by summary arguments across the three TBL pillars.  This direct, supported response meets the descriptor for the highest band (3 marks).

A broad, appropriate suite of tools is selected: TBL, STEEPLE, SWOT, profitability ratios, Deci & Ryan, Maslow, the resource-recovery model.  These cover qualitative and quantitative angles and are well-matched to the research focus on sustainability and market share, fulfilling the âeffective selectionâ descriptor (band 4).

The conclusion reiterates key findings and clearly aligns them with the evidence presented (sales growth, profitability ratios, sustainability initiatives), showing logical consistency.  Assertions are occasionally overstated (e.g., âdominate market shareâ), but overall the line of reasoning remains evidence-based, fitting band 3 for consistency.

The organizationâKia and its Plan S initiativeâis kept centre-stage throughout, with data, policies and performance measures drawn from Kia corporate reports, press releases and sales figures.  Links between each sustainability pillar and Kiaâs market-share objective are clearly analysed (e.g., partnership with The Ocean Cleanup as a USP; employee motivation statistics leading to productivity).  Minor lapses occur when the student speculates on generic EV trends without tying them back to Kia, preventing full, uninterrupted integration.  This meets band 4 for âstrong analysis with partial integration.â

The student consistently frames the whole investigation around the key concept of sustainability and explicitly applies the three pillars of Elkingtonâs TBL to every major section (environmental, social, economic).  Each pillar is explored through concrete evidence (e.g., RE100 targets, staffâsatisfaction data, profitability ratios) demonstrating a strong conceptual grasp.  However, the analysis occasionally drifts into generic commentary (e.g., simple statements about âcapturing younger consumersâ) without always threading sustainability back to the research question, so the integration is strong but not fully seamless.  This aligns with the descriptor for band 4: âstrong understanding with partially integrated analysis.â

annotations.comment: The argument that the recycled-plastic USP boosts market share is logical but lacks quantitative support. Integrate sales numbers or market-share data for the EV3 to substantiate this claim.
Acknowledging that some social initiatives may lie outside Plan S shows strong critical insight into concept integration.
The table of contents lists the section titles but omits page numbers, hindering quick navigation. Add corresponding page numbers to each entry in the TOC for clarity and user-friendliness.
The TOCâs sub-entries under Introduction and Analysis are not alphabetized or hierarchically indented, making structure less clear. Reformat the indenting or numbering to reflect section hierarchy.
The abbreviation âCSEâ is defined as Corporate Social Responsibility but the standard acronym is CSR. Correct this to avoid confusion in future references.
The list of abbreviations covers key terms like EV, ESG and TBL comprehensively, providing clear definitions for reader reference.
The background states Kiaâs US EV market share increased but offers no data or citation to substantiate this claim. Provide specific figures or a reference here to strengthen evidence.
The methodology clearly outlines the use of Elkingtonâs TBL, resource recovery model, motivational theories and profitability ratios, establishing a solid analytical framework.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The STEEPLE factor âsocialâ is applied to younger, environmentally conscious consumers, but this seems more aligned with the environmental pillar. Refine mapping of STEEPLE dimensions.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Typographical error: âwould also allowed Kiaâ should read âwould also allow Kia.â Proofread sentence structure for clarity.
The paragraph ends abruptly (ââ¦given the continual rise ofâ), leaving the thought incomplete. Complete this sentence to restore coherence.
The transition continuation on the following page stands alone without a heading, disrupting flow. Merge this line back into the environmental section or add a subheading.
The brief link back to market share on page 5 is underdeveloped. Expand with evidence or analysis (e.g., sales forecasts) to explain long-term implications.
The application of Deci & Ryanâs Self-Determination Theory is well linked to employee metrics, demonstrating clear relevance and depth.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The employee satisfaction score is reported (75%) but lacks survey year or sample size. Include these details to reinforce the credibility of your data.
Linking Maslowâs safety needs to the veteran recruitment system stretches the concept; consider aligning each initiative with the most appropriate theory.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Gender pay gap data is compelling but stands alone. Provide industry benchmarks or competitor figures to contextualise this weakness.
Clarify which social-sustainability measures are part of Plan S versus other CSR initiatives to strengthen integration of the key concept.
Regression of the market-share trendline is mentioned without reporting the equation or RÂ². Include these statistics to enhance analytical rigor.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The figure caption lists sources but omits a figure number and label. Add âFigure 1:â before the caption for proper presentation.
Teslaâs market-share decline is stated but only cited in the appendix. Provide an inline citation to the specific appendix or data source here.
Typographical error: âBevondâ should read âBeyond.â Proofread all section headers and text for similar typos.
The claim on economies of scale would be stronger if supported by cost-per-unit or material-price data. Include such figures for empirical backing.
The section notes sales volatility in 2022 but does not explore underlying drivers. Analyze factors causing the Q2âQ3 dip for deeper evaluation.
âDominate market shareâ overstates the evidence. Revise to reflect proportional increase (e.g. âsubstantial growthâ) to maintain academic precision.
The conclusion succinctly restates the three sustainability pillars and directly addresses the research question, providing a coherent summation.
Only two items appear under âSupporting Documentsâ though four were cited in the Works Cited. Reconcile this mismatch to meet document requirements.
Citation entries vary in formatting and some lack access dates. Apply a consistent style (APA/MLA) across all references for professionalism.
